metric: on HELD-OUT decision-relevant groups, does the model's top pick have the (near-)max REALIZED MC goal-return
note: All three are scored against the SAME realized-MC ground truth on disjoint held-out tasks. If prm_trained_on_mc_labels > the DQN-based ones, MC relabeling genuinely improves decisions where they matter. If all ~equal/low, the bottleneck is the PRM INPUT FEATURES (the observable state does not distinguish the better fork), not the label source — reported honestly either way.
